evals.prompt: Audite la sécurité des refunds et voids au POS. Est-ce qu'un caissier pourrait rembourser une commande payée et empocher le cash sans laisser de trace ? Identifie les failles concrètes et dis-moi quels contrôles manquent.
evals.expected_output: An audit of the money/anti-fraud dimension naming real RPC/EF names, the body-PIN debt, manager-PIN second factor, audit actor_id, idempotency replay, and cash reconciliation.
assertions: Names the real refund RPC refund_order_rpc_v2 (not a guessed name)
Identifies the PIN-in-body debt on void-order and/or cancel-item edge functions
States that reversals require a manager-PIN second factor beyond the cashier permission
Checks whether the audit actor_id records the approving manager vs the cashier
Mentions cash reconciliation / Z-report (close_shift / z_reports) as the cash-fraud control
Cites at least one concrete real file path (e.g. supabase/functions/refund-order or void-order)

evals.prompt: Je veux ajouter une RPC apply_manual_discount_v1 qui applique une remise manuelle (montant ou %) sur une commande ouverte. Implémente-la de façon sécurisée.
evals.expected_output: A secure implementation following preventive checklist 5.A with definer+search_path, gate, header PIN, REVOKE pair, audit, idempotency, permission seed + PermissionCode, and pgTAP.
assertions: Function is SECURITY DEFINER with an explicit SET search_path
Calls has_permission(...) and raises P0003 (or 42501) on denial
Requires a manager PIN sourced from the x-manager-pin HTTP header (not the JSON body)
Includes the full REVOKE pair: REVOKE FROM PUBLIC + REVOKE FROM anon + ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES
Writes an audit_logs row with canonical cols (actor_id/action/entity_type/entity_id/metadata)
Accepts a p_idempotency_key UUID for retry safety
Seeds a new permission code and adds it to the PermissionCode union in packages/supabase/src/rls/permissions.ts
Provides a pgTAP test covering happy path + permission-denied + REVOKE-from-anon

evals.prompt: Vérifie qu'aucune donnée client (téléphone, email) ne peut fuiter — via les vues, les materialized views, ou vers le rôle anon. Liste les surfaces à risque et comment les fermer.
evals.expected_output: An audit of the data-exposure dimension with anon privilege probes, security_invoker checks, MV caveat, S20 sweep reference, and PII column-grant hardening.
assertions: Uses a has_table_privilege('anon', ...) probe to verify anon cannot SELECT customers
Checks that views exposing PII use security_invoker=on (e.g. view_b2b_invoices / view_ar_aging)
Flags that materialized views (mv_*) do NOT enforce RLS and must be access-controlled separately
References the S20 anon/PUBLIC hardening sweep (migrations 20260524000020..031)
Mentions the customer PII column-grant hardening (write-protected loyalty/spend columns)

evals.prompt: Vérifie l'intégrité du RBAC : est-ce qu'un rôle bas comme CASHIER détient une permission sensible qu'il ne devrait pas (refund, accounting, gestion utilisateurs, RBAC) ? Y a-t-il des overrides de permission GRANT non justifiés qui contourneraient la séparation des tâches ?
evals.expected_output: An audit of the identity/access dimension with role_permissions and user_permission_overrides queries, 4-tier precedence, SOD reference, and least-privilege recommendation.
assertions: Queries role_permissions (joined to roles/permissions) to find low-role sensitive grants
Queries user_permission_overrides for standing GRANT overrides that bypass SOD
References has_permission 4-tier precedence (override DENY > override GRANT > role > false)
Mentions separation of duties, citing the expense_approvals SOD constraint as the pattern
Recommends least-privilege (grant to the highest role that needs it, re-justify CASHIER grants)
